'''Foul Moudama''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Talz/Legends|Talz]] from [[Alzoc III/Legends|Alzoc III]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] as a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] during the last years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Biography==
===Life as Jedi===
Born on the isolated, uncharted planet [[Alzoc III/Legends|Alzoc III]], Foul Moudama was discovered to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] near birth and taken to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]; one of the only members of his species to leave his mysterious homeworld during the era. While knowledge of his species and culture were limited even within the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Temple's vast archives]], Moudama managed to adapt to life on Coruscant and passed through the [[Jedi academy|Temple academy]] after years of training. Selected by a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] to train as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], Moudama continued to seek knowledge wherever he could and grow stronger in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Crafting for himself a blue-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], Moudama emerged from his [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]] as a fully ranking [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]].<ref name="db" />

During his independent studies as a Knight, Moudama continued to hone his skills with a lightsaber. Despite his hulking size, he was quite agile and swift of foot, a trait that often caught adversaries off-guard.<ref name="db" /> A firm devotee of the [[Living Force/Legends|Living Force]], Moudama's serene nature garnered the respect of the Jedi he worked closely with; though he was not especially well known throughout the Order, even by members of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]].<ref name="LoE">''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]''</ref> Serving during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] that was brewing between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Moudama was active when the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted across the galaxy. Reporting to Coruscant to receive the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] within the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], Moudama led many battles throughout the war as a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]].<ref name="db" />

===Confronting Grevious===
[[File:Foul_Palpatine.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Jedi Moudama carries Chancellor Palpatine while fleeing General Grievous.]]

As the end of the war drew near,<ref name="Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith">{{Film|III}}</ref> Moudama was stationed at the Coruscant Temple when [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] launched a [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|surprise assault]] on the capital planet. On the orders of [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], Jedi Moudama joined fellow Knights [[Roron Corobb/Legends|Roron Corobb]], [[B'ink Utrila]], and [[Roth-Del Masona]] in heading to the bunker that was to house [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during the assault. When the four Jedi arrived, they met with Masters [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]] and [[Stass Allie/Legends|Stass Allie]] who had thus far been responsible for safeguarding the leader of the Republic. Under Ti's command, the four Knights took the Chancellor and his [[Red Guard]]smen down into the bunker while Ti and Allie remained behind to defend the entrance. While Ti and Allie were distracted Grievous led a group of [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|MagnaGuards]] into the bunker and attacked Moudama and his companions. While the Red Guards were all slain by the MagnaGuards, the four Jedi engaged the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] General in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. While Grievous toyed with them for a few moments, he [[Sai cha/Legends|decapitated]] Jedi Masona before cutting down Jedi Corobb, leaving Moudama and Utrila alone. Putting forth his best effort, Moudama charged the General but lost his lightsaber arm and then his left leg before being disemboweled. Utrila died shortly after, and Grievous escaped with the Chancellor.<ref name="LoE" />

===Legacy===
Before leaving [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] took all the Jedi's ligthsabers. Not long after their deaths, Master Allie came upon the bodies, noticing that all of the Jedi were missing their lightsabers.<ref name="LoE" />

==Powers and abilities==
Wise and powerful, Jedi Foul Moudama had enhanced eyesight thanks to his dual set of eyes. An accomplished swordsman and respected for his fluid lightsaber technique, he was quite strong, able to carry Chancellor Palpatine in one arm while fending off MagnaGuards and [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] himself with the other.<ref name="db" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:FoulMoudamaDeath-CW25.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Foul being struck down by [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] in the animated cartoon.]]
Foul Moudama was created in response to a poll conducted by cartoonnetwork.com. The poll inquired whether Moudama, [[Roron Corobb/Legends|Roron Corobb]] or [[Voolvif Monn]] should be featured in ''Clone Wars''. Though Voolvif Monn was the winner, all three were eventually created. However, his appearance sparked some controversy as his species, the [[Talz/Legends|Talz]], were previously established to have not been discovered by the galactic population until the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Ultimate Alien Anthology">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref> To rectify this, Moudama's entry in the [[Databank (original)|Databank]] explains that his origins were mysterious and few knew where it was he came from.<ref name="db" />

His name, inspired by ''Foul Moudamas'' (spelling varies), a Middle-Eastern fava bean dish, according to the ''Clone Wars'' DVD commentary,<ref name="Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume Two">[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume Two|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume Two]]</ref> does not appear in the book, ''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]'', though it has been retconned that he does appear in this novel. Within these pages, Moudama's death appears as it does in this article.<ref name="LoE" /> However, [[Star Wars: Clone Wars|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'']] provides a different look at the confrontation between Moudama and Grievous.<ref name=":0">{{CW|25}}</ref> Instead of fleeing into the bunker with Jedi Utrila and Masona,<ref name="LoE" /> Moudama stands with Corobb and Master Shaak Ti while carrying the Chancellor under his arm.<ref name="Clone Wars Chapter 24">{{CW|24}}</ref> After a long chase to the bunker and an extended duel with the cyborg, Moudama is cut down alongside his [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] counterpart.<ref name=":0" />

An action figure of Foul Moudama was created as part of the [[2006]] Hasbro Saga Collection. A re-paint of the POTF2 [[Muftak/Legends|Muftak]] figure, but given a Jedi tunic and boots, as well as a slight variation on the paint job.
